reviews.review_msg: Prof. Linley is super kind. She gives a ton of feedback on assignments, although takes a long time to get back. I found her lectures vary draining and found it easier to learn the difficult and dense material on my own. It wad also a bit annoying that we would consistently go over time for class but overall assignments are easy and she is great!

Kind, welcoming, and always open to questions. Would recommend staying after class to go over questions or potential ideas for assignments, because she really puts in the effort to help you understand. Lectures were confusing at times, but discussions with her always helped clear them up.
One of my favourite profs at SFU

This prof is great! If you do the readings, participate in class once and a while, and do the assigned work, you will do well. Lectures can be a bit slow at times but she is caring and helpful. I'd recommend her classes but be ready to write proper English papers and do the readings.

Lots of reading for this class, often reading entire novels to only discuss them for one week. However, Margaret always had interesting supplemental articles to go with the books/poetry that enhanced the learning experience. Very discussion heavy, but she didn't force people to contribute if they weren't comfortable doing so.

First of all, Margaret is an insanely caring prof who wants to see her students succeed. She was very accessible when I reached out to her outside of class and she had very clear marking criteria. The readings were heavy at times, but expected for an upper div course. Overall, Margaret is a great prof and if you put in the effort you will do great!

This is the second English course I have taken at SFU and let me just say this prof was amazing. I truly enjoyed her lectures, as they were not excessively long and they were straight to the point. Also, she was extremely helpful through email if you ever had any questions.

I won't speak too much about her teaching style or how much she cares. This was an asynchronous remote class, I never once talked to her. I will say the materials in her class introduced me to some amazaing literature, some classic, some contemporary. And she really awakened a whole new perspective on nature, literatue and culture.

Dr. Linley goes out of her way to help students understand if they ask questions. She was super accessible outside of class and seemed like she really wanted people to succeed. The readings were pretty dense but she would give lots of information during lectures to help make the material more digestible.
Her lectures are completely off topic to what you will be graded on. No aspects of writing are given, she just summarizes the books you are already going to read.
